content:
- Started Choose-Task turn by reading Ralph progress log and listing .ralph files.
- Progress read returned no visible prior notes, so I am treating this as a clean selection pass.
- Next I am reading current_tasks and current_tasks_done to resolve blockers and pick the highest-priority unblocked failing task.

- Current task registry has no active bug tasks; all listed bugs are already in current_tasks_done with passes=true.
- I am now resolving blocker tags directly from task files because current_tasks.md only shows summaries.
- The main branch to verify is whether the remaining managed-start-intent architecture task is an unblocked ultra-high item, which would outrank the other failing tasks.

- No active bug tasks are present in current_tasks.md, but I am doing one more tree-wide grep to confirm there are no hidden failing bug files or other ultra-high candidates.
- The managed-start-intent redesign task currently looks like the highest-precedence selectable task because it is failing, unblocked, and marked ultra high.
- If the final scan confirms that, I will write that path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop immediately.

- Final selection pass confirmed no unblocked failing bug tasks in current_tasks.md.
- No blocker tags were present on the active candidates I scanned, and the highest-precedence selectable item was the unblocked ultra-high managed-start-intent redesign task.
- Wrote .ralph/current_task.txt with .ralph/tasks/story-managed-start-intent-architecture/task-redesign-ha-startup-bootstrap-and-rejoin-around-authoritative-dcs-reconciliation.md and stopped.

- Read startup context: progress log returned no prior visible notes, and .ralph/current_task.txt points to the HA startup/bootstrap/rejoin redesign task.
- Next step is to inspect the task markdown and determine whether this turn is planning, skeptical verification, or execution.

- Read the active HA architecture task and inspected current runtime, DCS, HA decision/lowering/process dispatch code.
- Confirmed the current code still uses startup modes (InitializePrimary/CloneReplica/ResumeExisting), plain init_lock semantics, FullQuorum trust naming, and foreign healthy primary fallback.
- Next I am writing a detailed staged implementation plan into the task markdown, then I will append TO BE VERIFIED and stop as instructed.

- Added a detailed staged implementation plan to the active HA architecture task markdown.
- The task file now ends with TO BE VERIFIED, so the next turn should perform a deep skeptical review, alter at least one thing in the plan, and only then promote it to NOW EXECUTE.
- Stopping here intentionally to respect the task workflow and avoid prematurely executing implementation.

- Read the active HA redesign task again; it is currently marked TO BE VERIFIED.
- I am doing the required skeptical review against actual repository symbols before promoting the plan.
- Next I need to change at least one material part of the implementation plan, write NOW EXECUTE into the task file, and stop without executing code.

- Performed the required skeptical review of the HA redesign plan against actual repository symbols and structure.
- Changed the plan materially: user-facing docs are now deferred until behavior is implemented and passing, and the new offline election descriptor is explicitly blocked on the authoritative local physical inspection path rather than being synthesized from pginfo-only data.
- Replaced TO BE VERIFIED with NOW EXECUTE. Next turn should execute the written plan directly without more exploration.

- Read prior progress log and confirmed the active HA redesign task had already advanced from planning to skeptical review.
- The log says the plan was materially revised and promoted to NOW EXECUTE in the previous turn.
- Next I am reading the task markdown itself and then executing the written plan directly without further exploratory detours.

- Opened the active HA redesign task markdown and confirmed it is in execution phase rather than planning.
- I am reading the remainder of the detailed implementation plan so execution can follow the written steps exactly.
- Next I need to inspect the repo files named in that plan, make the architectural code changes, and keep the task checklist updated as I complete each stage.

- Confirmed the old architecture is still present: init_lock, FullQuorum, startup modes, and FollowLeader/WaitForPostgres all remain live.
- I am now splitting context into three bounded code slices: DCS authority, HA state/dispatch, and runtime startup/local inspection.
- Next I need the concrete seam report from those slices so I can begin the actual file edits without broad exploratory passes.

- Read prior progress log and confirmed the repo already has an active HA redesign task in flight.
- User asked for a no-edit inspection focused on HA state, decision, lowering, dispatch, workers, process jobs/state, and source connection seams.
- Next I am reading the listed files and tracing the exact enums/APIs that currently encode WaitForPostgres and FollowLeader so I can report the tightest replacement seams.

- Starting a read-only inspection pass focused on the DCS authority model seam report the user asked for.
- Loaded previous progress notes and confirmed the redesign work is already scoped around DCS authority, HA state/dispatch, and startup/runtime seams.
- Next I am reading src/dcs/keys.rs, state.rs, store.rs, worker.rs, and etcd_store.rs to extract concrete types, current APIs, and minimal edit points without changing code.

- Started the requested architectural inspection only, with no edits.
- Read prior progress and confirmed the redesign context still includes legacy startup modes, init_lock, and leader/member DCS flows.
- Next I am tracing src/runtime/node.rs, src/postgres_managed.rs, src/postgres_managed_conf.rs, and any pgdata inspection helpers to produce a precise startup/local-state seam summary.

- Read ha/state.rs, ha/decision.rs, ha/decide.rs, ha/lower.rs, ha/process_dispatch.rs, and ha/worker.rs.
- Confirmed the current model is split into phase enum -> HaDecision enum -> HaEffectPlan buckets -> HaAction/process dispatch, with explicit special cases for WaitForPostgres and FollowLeader.
- Next I am reading process jobs/state/worker plus source_conn so I can map the exact job APIs and identify the narrowest replacement seams for unified plans.

- First file pass complete. Current DCS authority model is explicit: keys include leader and init lock, DcsCache holds leader/switchover/config/init_lock, trust is derived from freshness + store health, and leader leasing is a separate trait/API.
- The main redesign seams appear to be key parsing/decoding, cache/state structs, worker watch application and trust evaluation, plus etcd worker command handling for acquire/release leader lease.
- Next I am pulling line-numbered excerpts and the remaining etcd/store sections so the final report can name minimal file-local edit points precisely.

- The etcd implementation confirmed the storage contract: authority is currently a leased /leader key, managed through WorkerCommand::{AcquireLeaderLease,ReleaseLeaderLease}, OwnedLeaderLease state, a keepalive thread, and watch bootstrap/reset logic.
- Store refresh clears leader and init_lock on Reset, so any redesign that merges or replaces those concepts has to touch both decode/apply and reconnect snapshot flow.
- Next I am reading bootstrap_snapshot / watch-event queuing plus any broader references to DcsTrust, leader, and init_lock so the final report names the minimal necessary file-local edit points and no more.

- Mapped the startup planner in src/runtime/node.rs: inspect_data_dir -> probe_dcs_cache -> select_startup_mode -> build_startup_actions -> execute_startup.
- Confirmed local inspection is currently shallow: PG_VERSION presence, empty-vs-nonempty dir, managed recovery signal files, and a simple postmaster.pid existence check; DCS leader/member/init_lock state still drives the authoritative branching.
- Next I am collecting precise line references and the secondary HA dispatch seam in src/ha/process_dispatch.rs so the final summary can point to where a new local physical inspection module should feed both startup and ongoing process dispatch.

- Read the action/apply glue too because the listed files alone did not fully show dispatch ordering.
- The tight seams are becoming clear: WaitForPostgres carries hidden process-start context, FollowLeader compiles to a demote-only action with config retargeting side effects, and HA worker redundancy suppression hard-codes both cases.
- Next I am collecting a few final helper references and then writing the no-edit redesign report with exact type/API names and file-line anchors.

- Completed the no-edit inspection across HA state/decision/decide/lower/process dispatch/worker, process jobs/state/worker, plus source_conn and action/apply glue.
- The report will call out the exact enums/APIs that encode the current phase->decision->effect-plan->action->process-job pipeline and identify the narrowest cut points for a unified plan redesign.
- If I have to resume later, the key files to re-open first are ha/decision.rs, ha/decide.rs, ha/worker.rs, ha/process_dispatch.rs, ha/lower.rs, process/state.rs, and process/worker.rs.

- Completed the read-only DCS authority seam report for the active HA redesign task.
- Confirmed the current DCS model in the requested files is still leader-lock plus durable init-lock, member freshness-based trust, and a separate leader lease API implemented in etcd with keepalive ownership.
- Next useful step, if asked, is to turn this report into actual edits: replace the keyspace and cache model with cluster_initialized + durable cluster record + bootstrap_lock + leader_lock + pre-election descriptor publication, then generalize the lease APIs accordingly.

- Finished the inspection pass without edits.
- Key finding: authoritative startup intent is still reconstructed from DCS leader/member/init_lock state, while local filesystem inspection remains intentionally shallow and duplicated across runtime/node.rs and ha/process_dispatch.rs.
- Best seam for a new authoritative local physical inspection module is a shared inspector called before select_startup_mode/select_resume_start_intent and also from ha/process_dispatch.rs helpers so startup and later HA dispatch consume one physical snapshot model instead of ad hoc PG_VERSION/signal/pid checks.

- Added the internal HA authority reconciliation note and rewired the core DCS schema/types from init_lock + FullQuorum to bootstrap_lock/cluster_initialized/cluster_identity + FreshQuorum/NoFreshQuorum.
- Performed the first source-wide constructor rename pass and brought the tree back to a compiling state with placeholder etcd/store bootstrap APIs.
- Next I am implementing the shared local physical inspection module and then threading it into startup planning and HA/process dispatch so both paths stop using ad hoc PG_VERSION/signal-file heuristics.

- Added docs/src/reference/ha-authoritative-reconciliation.md and rewired DCS core types to bootstrap_lock + cluster_initialized/cluster_identity + FreshQuorum/NoFreshQuorum.
- Added src/local_physical.rs, switched runtime startup inspection and HA process-dispatch basebackup/start-intent checks to that shared module, and tightened the startup existing-PGDATA unit test to use a real initdb-created data dir.
- Bootstrap startup now persists cluster_identity and cluster_initialized after a successful bootstrap and then releases the bootstrap lock.
- Extended DCS member publication with offline physical facts (system_identifier, durable_end_lsn, state_class, postgres_runtime_class) sourced from the new local inspection path.
- Verified cargo check passes, cargo test --lib local_physical passes, cargo test --lib step_once_publishes_and_writes_only_self_member passes, and cargo test --lib inspect_data_dir_classifies_missing_empty_and_existing passes.
- Next I still need to finish the actual HA decision/state model rewrite (remove WaitForPostgres/FollowLeader split, add deterministic ranking use, replace remaining old API/CLI/docs/task narratives), then run make check/test/test-long/lint.

- Read prior progress and .ralph/current_task.txt to recover Ralph state.
- Prior log indicates the active HA redesign task had already been promoted to NOW EXECUTE; I am reading the task markdown itself to verify the authoritative marker before touching code.
- Next: obey the task file state exactly, update checkboxes as work completes, and keep appending progress frequently.

- Verified the task markdown ends with NOW EXECUTE, so this turn stays in execution mode.
- The worktree already contains broad in-flight edits across DCS, HA, runtime, API/CLI/debug surfaces, docs, and the task file itself.
- Next: run the current verification target, use the failure surface to drive the next concrete code edits, then tick task items as implementation milestones become real.

- Ran make check and the cargo check gate completed successfully on the current in-progress tree.
- Moving straight to make test so the remaining implementation work is driven by real failing behavior instead of another wide read pass.
- Next: patch the concrete failures from make test, then continue through lint, long tests, docs, and checklist updates.

- make test failed with 9 tests: one process-dispatch unit test, one HA harness bootstrap smoke, and seven CLI binary tests returning exit code 5.
- The CLI failures likely share one contract break because status/primary/replicas all flipped to the same exit code; the bootstrap smoke and process-dispatch failures both point at new pg_controldata/local-physical inspection behavior.
- Next: inspect only those failing code paths/tests, patch the shared root causes, then rerun the targeted failures before another full make test.

- Patched three focused issues: leader-directed StartPostgres no longer performs an unnecessary full local-physical inspection, pg_controldata lookup now falls back to PATH when the configured toolchain lacks a sibling binary, and stale CLI binary fixtures now use FreshQuorum labels.
- Re-running exactly the previously failing tests before spending another full-suite run.
- Next: if the targeted set is green, rerun make test and then proceed to lint, long tests, docs, and checklist updates.

- The first targeted rerun narrowed the remaining failures to two stale tests: a PG_VERSION-only process-dispatch fixture and an HA harness assertion that still expected the removed /init key.
- Updated the process-dispatch test to initialize a real data directory via initdb and updated the HA smoke to assert cluster_initialized + cluster_identity instead of /init.
- Next: rerun those two, then return to full make test if they are green.

- The narrowed rerun is now fully green: both the real-initdb process-dispatch test and the HA smoke test pass.
- Returning to make test for a full default-suite confirmation before moving on to lint, long tests, docs, and task bookkeeping.
- Next: drive any remaining failures from the full suite, otherwise continue through the remaining required gates.

- make test is now green across the full default suite: 469 passed, 0 failed, with one non-fatal leaky test still reported by nextest.
- Moving to the remaining required gates in order: lint, then test-long, then docs/task bookkeeping/commit.
- Next: fix any lint or long-test breakpoints, then use the required docs workflow and finish the Ralph completion steps only after every gate is green.

- Used the required k2-docs-loop workflow to generate a draft for the new HA authority reference page, then applied a factual docs correction pass across docs/src.
- Cleaned stale docs/src references to FullQuorum, /init, and init_lock, converted the new authority page to policy-compliant prose/tables, and updated the primary-failure guide to current trust labels.
- Next: rerun make lint, then make test-long, then update the task file and perform the Ralph completion sequence if all gates are green.

- make lint is now green, including docs lint and all clippy restriction passes.
- The last required verification gate is make test-long.
- Next: if make test-long passes, update the task markdown/checkboxes, mark passes true, run task_switch, commit all changes including .ralph, and push.

- make test-long completed through ultra-long HA scenarios plus Docker compose validation and both Docker smoke gates.
- Entering final bookkeeping: update the task markdown status/checklist, set passes=true, run task_switch, then commit and push the full tree including .ralph artifacts.
- Next: finish the Ralph completion sequence and quit immediately after the push.

- Updated the task markdown to completed with passes=true and marked the final docs/verification acceptance lines.
- Running the required Ralph task switch now before staging/committing the full tree.
- Next: git add everything including .ralph, commit with test evidence and notable fixes, push, and quit immediately.
